title: Big Technology AI Summit (full): Greg Brockman, Mike Krieger, Aaron Levie & Friends of The Podcast
summary: Live summit featuring AI leaders discussing federal regulation of frontier models, the Anthropic Fable and Mythos recall, AI infrastructure buildout, enterprise budget priorities, and competitive dynamics between OpenAI and Anthropic. Speakers debate whether regulation accelerates China's advantage and whether compute or outcomes will dominate the next phase of AI development.
writeup: This full recording of the Big Technology AI Summit, hosted by Alex Kantrowitz, gathers AI leaders including Greg Brockman, Mike Krieger, and Aaron Levie for live discussion of frontier model development and its policy stakes. Sessions cover federal regulation of frontier models, the Anthropic Fable and Mythos recall, AI infrastructure buildout, and how enterprises set budget priorities. Speakers debate whether regulation hands China a competitive advantage and whether raw compute or measurable outcomes will define AI's next phase, alongside the rivalry between OpenAI and Anthropic. The throughline is a tension between safety-driven regulation and the cost and competitive pressures shaping enterprise adoption.
